info.title: Itch.io API
info.description: The itch.io server-side API provides authenticated access to user profiles, uploaded games, download key validation, purchase lookup, and build version retrieval. Authentication is via API key or short-lived JWT tokens using the Authorization Bearer header. Responses are JSON with snake_case naming and RFC 3339 dates.

tags.name: Profile
tags.description: Operations related to the authenticated user's profile

tags.name: Games
tags.description: Operations related to itch.io game pages

tags.name: Uploads
tags.description: Operations related to game uploads and downloads

tags.name: Collections
tags.description: Operations related to user game collections

get.description: Returns information about the user the current credentials belong to.
Lists the games the authenticated user develops (i.e. can edit).
Lists the download keys the authenticated account owns.
Lists the collections associated to the authenticated profile.
Lists the bundles the current user owns. Each BundleKey has its Bundle field populated. Deduped across multiple purchases of the same bundle; sale bundles are currently excluded; capped at 100.
Lists builds across all games the current user develops. The response is normalized: each build carries scalar GameID/UploadID/UserID, and the referenced games/uploads/users are returned once each.

parameters.name: state
parameters.description: Filter by build state. One of: live, processing, failed. Empty for all.

parameters.name: include_totals
parameters.description: If set to 1, include aggregate totals in the response.

parameters.name: started_build_ids
parameters.description: Comma-separated build IDs in the started state to surface in the listing.

get.description: Retrieves a single game by ID.
Lists the uploads for a game that are accessible with the current API key and game credentials.

post.description: Creates a new download session for more accurate download analytics. Multiple file downloads (patch, signature, etc.) for a single game upgrade should share one session.

get.description: Retrieves information about a single upload by ID.
Lists recent builds for a given upload.
Retrieves scanned archive metadata for an upload.
Retrieves info about a single build by ID (consumer-side endpoint).
Returns the complete list of builds to go through to upgrade from one version to another.
Retrieves scanned archive metadata for a build.
Retrieves info about a single user by ID.
Retrieves a single collection by ID.
Retrieves a page of a collection's games.
Retrieves a page of a bundle's games. The current user must own the bundle.

post.description: Returns a DownloadKey for a game owned via a bundle purchase, materializing one if the bundle defers download key creation. Atomic and idempotent.

game_id.description: ID of the game to claim from the bundle

get.description: Performs a text search for games (or any project type). Games must be published and not deindexed.
Performs a text search for users.

post.description: Attempts to log a user into itch.io with their username (or email) and password. The response may indicate that TOTP or recaptcha is needed.
Verifies a TOTP (two-factor authentication) code after the initial login step.

token.description: Token from the initial login response

get.description: Requests the status of the wharf build infrastructure.
Returns a list of channels for a game.
Returns information about a given channel for a given game.

post.description: Creates a new build for a given user/game:channel, with an optional user version.

hidden.description: If true, the build is hidden

source.description: Source that initiated the push (e.g. cli, butlerd, app)

get.description: Retrieves info about a single build by ID via the owner-side wharf endpoint.
Returns a list of files associated to a build.

post.description: Creates a new build file entry for a build.
Marks the end of the upload for a build file. Validates that the file size in storage matches the provided size.

get.description: Returns a series of events associated with a given build.

post.description: Associates a new build event (e.g. log message) to a build.
Marks a given build as failed with an error message.

fatal.description: If true, the build cannot be retried

post.description: Marks a given build as having failed to rediff (optimize).

bearerAuth.description: API key or JWT token issued by itch.io

downloadKeyId.name: download_key_id
downloadKeyId.description: Download key ID for accessing paid content

Unauthorized.description: Authentication is required or credentials are invalid

User.description: Represents an itch.io account with basic profile info

id.description: Site-wide unique identifier generated by itch.io

displayName.description: The user's display name; may contain spaces and unicode characters

developer.description: Has the user opted into creating games?

pressUser.description: Is the user part of itch.io's press program?

url.description: The address of the user's page on itch.io

coverUrl.description: User's avatar URL; may be a GIF

stillCoverUrl.description: Static version of user's avatar; only set if the main cover URL is a GIF

Game.description: Represents a page on itch.io; could be a game, tool, comic, etc.

url.description: Canonical address of the game's page on itch.io

stillCoverUrl.description: Non-gif cover URL; only set if the main cover is a GIF

publishedAt.description: Date the game was published; empty if not currently published

minPrice.description: Price in cents of a dollar

hasDemo.description: Does this game have a demo available?

inPressSystem.description: Is this game part of the itch.io press system?

width.description: Width of the initial viewport in pixels

height.description: Height of the initial viewport in pixels

fullscreen.description: Whether a fullscreen button should be shown

Platforms.description: Describes which OS/architectures a game or upload is compatible with

Sale.description: Describes a discount for a game

rate.description: Discount rate in percent; can be negative (reverse sales)

Upload.description: A downloadable file; may be wharf-enabled for versioned channel-based distribution

channelName.description: Name of the wharf channel for this upload, if wharf-enabled

preorder.description: Is this upload a pre-order placeholder?

demo.description: Is this upload a free demo?

Build.description: Contains information about a specific build

parentBuildId.description: Identifier of the build before this one on the same channel, or -1 if initial

BuildFile.description: Contains information about a build file (archive, signature, patch, etc.)

Channel.description: Contains information about a channel and its current status

name.description: Name of the channel (e.g. windows-64-beta, osx-universal)

CollectionGame.description: Represents a game's membership in a collection

Bundle.description: A collection of games sold together as a single purchase

BundleGame.description: Represents a game's membership in a bundle

minPrice.description: Minimum price for this game inside the bundle in cents of a dollar

BundleKey.description: Records a profile's ownership of a Bundle via a purchase

DownloadKey.description: Allows downloading uploads for a game; typically generated at purchase time

APIKey.description: Grants access to the itch.io API within a certain scope

FileUploadSpec.description: Contains the info needed to upload one specific build file

BuildEvent.description: Describes something that happened while processing a build

UpgradePath.description: A series of builds for which sequential patches exist

uuid.description: UUID for the new download session

cookie.description: Session cookie key-value pairs for browser session
